steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Main Idea question. Two precision signals in the passage are decisive: (1) Rhoden’s team proposed (not confirmed) an explanation, and (2) the wobble could be due to a hidden ocean. The correct answer must preserve this tentative language and cover both the wobble and the ocean hypothesis.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Rhoden and colleagues were the first to confirm that several of Saturn's moons contain hidden oceans.: Two errors: the team proposed rather than confirmed anything, and only Mimas was studied — no other moons were confirmed to have oceans. ✗
  • Choice B — Research has failed to identify signs that there is an ocean hidden beneath the surface of Mimas.: Directly contradicts the text. The wobble is a sign that the team says could indicate a hidden ocean. ✗
  • Choice C — Rhoden and colleagues created a new computer model that identifies moons with hidden oceans without needing to analyze the moons' rotation.: Misrepresents the model. The computer model studied gravitational interactions to explain the wobbly rotation — rotation analysis is central, not bypassed. ✗
  • Choice D — Research has revealed that an oddity in the rotation of Mimas could be explained by an ocean hidden beneath its surface.: Matches the passage precisely: the wobble (rotation oddity) was revealed, and the hidden ocean is offered as a possible explanation. ✓
That matches Choice D — Research has revealed that an oddity in the rotation of Mimas could be explained by an ocean hidden beneath its surface..
